seo.meta_title: Better Auth vs Logto for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Better Auth (4.5★) vs Logto (4.1★) for Next.js. Compare Medium vs Medium learning curves, free tiers, and get our recommendation.
seo.og_title: Better Auth vs Logto: Next.js Auth Comparison (2026)
seo.og_description: We compared Better Auth and Logto head-to-head. Pricing: Free (Open Source) vs Free – $599/mo. Read our full analysis.

hero.headline: Better Auth vs Logto for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Better Auth and Logto — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: When comparing Better Auth and Logto for Next.js App Router authentication, the differences come down to your priorities. Better Auth leads with a 8/10 Next.js integration score and a medium learning curve. Logto scores 7/10 with a medium learning curve. Better Auth starts at Free (Open Source), while Logto starts at Free – $599/mo.
verdict_text: We recommend Better Auth for most Next.js App Router projects. Its medium learning curve and 30 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. Logto is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need yes, up to 5,000 mau or prefer Logto's approach to authentication.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, fully open source with self-hosting

pros: Fully open source with no vendor lock-in or usage limits
Self-hosted on your own infrastructure - complete data control
Growing community and active development on GitHub
cons: Requires database setup and self-hosting infrastructure
Smaller ecosystem than Auth0 or Clerk
Fewer pre-built UI components compared to managed alternatives

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

faq.question: Should I choose Better Auth or Logto for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Better Auth scores 8/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Logto scores 7/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Better Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, fully open source with self-hosting or Yes, up to 5,000 MAU is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Better Auth or Logto more affordable?
faq.answer: Better Auth starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source with self-hosting. Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Better Auth or Logto easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Better Auth has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 30 minutes. Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Logto is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Better Auth and Logto together?
faq.answer: Using both Better Auth and Logto in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Better Auth or Logto?
faq.answer: Better Auth has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 8/10, and a user rating of 4.5★. Logto has a Medium learning curve, 7/10, and 4.1★. On paper, Better Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
